# botyard_mcp_server (Resource)

Manages an organization-scoped Botyard MCP server. Two runtime kinds are supported: `container_image` (Botyard runs the server as a pod) and `managed_remote` (Botyard proxies to a vendor-hosted endpoint). Set the fields for the chosen `runtime_kind`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# A container-image MCP server: Botyard runs the server as a pod in-cluster.
resource "botyard_mcp_server" "search" {
  runtime_kind = "container_image"
  name         = "Web Search"
  image        = "ghcr.io/example/search-mcp:1.2.0"
  port         = 8080

  # Non-sensitive configuration.
  env_plaintext = {
    SEARCH_REGION = "us-east-1"
  }

  # Sensitive values are supplied as Runtime Vault key-path *pointers*, never as
  # raw secrets — Botyard resolves them at runtime.
  env_secret_refs = {
    SEARCH_API_KEY = "search.api_key"
  }
}

# A managed-remote MCP server: Botyard proxies to a vendor-hosted endpoint.
resource "botyard_mcp_server" "vendor" {
  runtime_kind = "managed_remote"
  name         = "Vendor MCP"
  endpoint_url = "https://mcp.vendor.example.com"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Human-readable label, unique within the organization.
- `runtime_kind` (String) Runtime kind: `container_image` or `managed_remote`. Changing this forces replacement.

### Optional

- `args` (List of String) Argv-style arguments (container_image only).
- `command` (List of String) Argv-style entrypoint override (container_image only).
- `description` (String) Optional free-form description.
- `endpoint_url` (String) Vendor-hosted MCP URL. Required when `runtime_kind = managed_remote`.
- `env_plaintext` (Map of String) Non-sensitive environment variables (container_image only).
- `env_secret_refs` (Map of String) Env-var name → secret_key_path references (container_image only). These are vault key-path pointers, not secret values.
- `image` (String) Container image reference. Required when `runtime_kind = container_image`.
- `pod_host_mode` (String) HTTP `Host` strategy for the in-cluster pod: `natural` (default) or `pod_localhost` (container_image only).
- `port` (Number) Container port for the streamable-http transport. Required when `runtime_kind = container_image`.
- `request_timeout_seconds` (Number) Per-server gateway request-timeout override (seconds). Null inherits the gateway default.
- `secret_file_mounts` (Map of String) Absolute container path → secret_key_path mounts, read-only (container_image only).
- `slug` (String) URL-safe identifier. Derived from the name on create when omitted, then preserved across updates unless explicitly set.
- `transport` (String) Wire transport. Defaults to `streamable_http`. Immutable — changing it forces replacement.

### Read-Only

- `config_generation` (Number) Monotonic config generation counter.
- `created_at` (String) Creation timestamp (RFC 3339).
- `desired_state` (String) Control-plane desired state.
- `id` (String) MCP server ID (UUID).
- `observed_state` (String) Observed lifecycle state.
- `org_id` (String) Organization ID that owns the server.
- `tool_count` (Number) Number of tools the server advertises.
- `updated_at` (String) Last-update timestamp (RFC 3339).

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# MCP servers are imported by their ID (UUID).
terraform import botyard_mcp_server.search 0c9f8b7a-6d5e-4c3b-2a1f-0e9d8c7b6a5f
```
